exemplar.title: Modelling the Deepseek Logo with curve fitting and regression

detailedExemplarData.title: Modelling the Deepseek Logo with curve fitting and regression

subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Effective use of headings, tables and figures to guide the reader
Most material directly supports the modelling goal
weaknesses: Occasional digressions and verbosity (long quotations, repeated statements)
Tables overflow pages and some figure references are mis-numbered
Formatting irregularities in mathematical steps detract from polish
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands sit comfortably in band 3: the exploration is coherent and logically developed, well organized with clear structure, and mostly concise with mostly relevant content. The presence of minor digressions and formatting issues prevents a top-band (4) rating, but overall the presentation is solid and earns 3 marks.

strengths: Correct use of mathematical symbols and definitions in most places
Effective use of multiple representations (equations, tables, Desmos graphs)
Good logical structure and clarity of explanations
weaknesses: Inconsistent formatting of notation and occasional missing algebraic steps
Repetitive representations and some blurry screenshots
Minor jumps in matrix operations reduce ultimate clarity
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands achieve band 3: mostly consistent use of appropriate notation, effective multiple representations, and good logical clarity. A few lapses (format inconsistencies, omitted steps) keep this criterion from the top band, so a score of 3 is appropriate.

strengths: Significant independent thinking in selecting and decomposing the logo
Clear personal motivation linked to interest in Deepseek and AI
Good creativity in manually applying GaussâJordan elimination
weaknesses: Approach follows standard curve-sketching procedures for much of the work
Reliance on Desmos for many equations limits originality
Opportunities to deepen personal reflections on choice of logo not fully exploited
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Each strand sits at band 2: evidence of independent thinking and personal approach is solid but not outstanding, and creativity is good but not exceptional. Thus, the criterion merits 2 marks.

strengths: Acknowledgement of limitations and imperfect fits in a dedicated paragraph
weaknesses: Reflection is limited to basic comments with no deep discussion of modelling choices
No quantitative evaluation (e.g., RÂ² values or residuals)
No exploration of future improvements or alternative strategies
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands are at band 1: only limited reflection, basic analysis, and limited evaluation of outcomes are present. Therefore, the criterion receives 1 mark.

strengths: Relevant mathematics (circle equations, polynomials, matrix methods) used appropriately
Demonstrates good understanding of circle geometry and polynomial fitting
Manual GaussâJordan elimination shows command of SL-level techniques
weaknesses: Minor computational and transcription errors (typos in coefficients)
Mathematics mostly at SL level with limited extension beyond course expectations
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Based on the three strands (relevance at band 4, level at band 3, understanding at band 3), the overall band is 3. The work is effective and appropriate but not fully commensurate or comprehensive, so the criterion earns 3 marks.

strandSelections.reasoning: Explanations of each step (e.g., finding perpendicular bisectors, applying Gaussian elimination) are clear and ordered, producing good logical structure.  Minor jumps (such as omitting several row-operations in matrices) reduce ultimate clarity, so the strand sits comfortably in band 3.

The student demonstrates correct understanding of circle geometry and polynomial fitting, and the manual matrix solution is largely accurate.  Minor computational slips and the absence of residual checks indicate good but not thorough command, placing the strand in band 3.

The student supplies equations, tables of parameters and many Desmos graphs, giving the reader multiple ways to visualise the mathematics.  The representations are effective but sometimes repetitive and a few screenshots are blurry, so the work meets, but does not exceed, the âeffectiveâ descriptor.

The report follows a clear narrative (introduction â methodology â results â limitations â conclusion).  Explanations generally flow from the collection of points to the construction of equations and finally to the assembled logo, so the reader can follow the mathematical story without major jumps.  However, the discussion occasionally digresses (e.g., long quotations of Penn State notes) and some sections repeat earlier statements, so the logical thread is good but not exemplary.

Manually applying Gauss-Jordan elimination and experimenting with degree choices shows creativity and initiative beyond merely using software.  Yet most other equations are generated directly by Desmos, limiting the overall originality to a âgoodâ rather than âexceptionalâ level.

The student briefly comments that designers probably did not think in terms of the derived equations, but offers little evaluation of statistical measures (e.g., RÂ² is promised but never shown).  The analysis therefore remains basic.
Apart from stating that the reconstruction is âdecently fittingâ, there is no quantitative comparison or consideration of future improvements, so the evaluation of outcomes is limited.

Reflection is confined to a short âLimitationsâ paragraph noting imperfect fits and rounding.  There is no deeper discussion of modelling choices or alternative strategies, so only limited reflection is evident.

Mathematical symbols (e.g., (x+a)Â²+(y+b)Â²=rÂ², inequalities for domains, matrix notation) are used correctly in most places and variables are defined.  A few slips occur (missing powers in the final polynomial, inconsistent boldface), preventing fully consistent top-band use.

The investigation is clearly motivated by the studentâs personal interest in Deepseek and Chinese language AI; the breakdown into arcs, polynomials and lines reflects a personal strategy.  However, the approach is not especially novel within curve-sketching IAs, so the strand reaches band 2.

Most material directly supports the goal of modelling the logo; irrelevant digressions are limited but present (e.g., background on Deepseek and lengthy definition of row-echelon form).  The write-up is fairly long and verbose, so it is âmostly relevant and conciseâ rather than âhighly conciseâ.

All mathematics employed (circle equations, linear functions, polynomial regression, matrix methods) is clearly relevant to modelling the logo, and each part of the picture is tackled with an appropriate technique.  The use is effective across the project.

Selecting an original logo and deciding which functions suit each section shows significant independent thinking, especially the manual matrix solution for one segment.  Still, much of the process follows textbook procedures, so the evidence is solid but not âoutstandingâ.

Headings, tables and figures are used effectively, and the reader can locate data, working and results quickly.  Nevertheless, several tables overflow pages and some figure references (e.g. Fig. 2 vs. matrix shown) are mis-numbered, showing that the structure, while clear, is not polished enough for the top band.

The mathematics is mostly at SL level; matrix row-reduction and fourth-degree polynomials meet HL expectations marginally, but there is little beyond that.  Hence the work is âcommensurate with course levelâ but not âfully commensurateâ or âcomprehensiveâ.

annotations.comment: General form \(ax^3+bx^2+cx+d\) is presented correctly; maintain consistent spacing around operators throughout to improve readability.
Check that the matrix entries in Table 2 match the previously derived equations; minor OCR errors (e.g. -0.137 vs -0.737) can lead to incorrect solutions.
The elimination steps are clearly numbered, but formatting irregularities in step 4 (âA, Rââ) should be corrected for professional presentation.
The final RREF in Fig. 6 cleanly isolates each coefficient, demonstrating mastery of GaussâJordan elimination.
The final polynomial is clearly stated, but there is a minor sign typo: the cubic coefficient should be \(-0.130086x^3\). Double-check your row-reduction arithmetic.
The introduction effectively contextualizes the investigation by linking Deepseekâs LLM to the mathematical reconstruction task, engaging the reader immediately.
The personal motivation is noted, but the student could deepen engagement by reflecting on why the whale logo resonates personally and how it influenced methodological choices.
Including the logo image provides a clear visual reference; ensure the figure is high resolution and numbered consistently throughout.
The methodology list is clear, but clarifying the sequence of software and manual steps would improve coherence and user reproducibility.
Specification of the logoâs scaling on Desmos is useful; consider explaining why width=10 and height=7.4 were chosen to justify coordinate ranges.
The rationale linking circle insufficiency to logo complexity is insightful, demonstrating personal recognition of mathematical limits.
The decision to combine polynomial and circular functions is logical; adding deeper reflection on alternative functional forms would strengthen critical analysis.
The circle equation is stated correctly using the Pythagorean theorem in $$ (x-a)^2+(y-b)^2=r^2 $$. Consider annotating each parameterâs geometric meaning directly in the text.
Providing three sample points is essential, but clarify the point-selection method and ensure they lie precisely on the curve to avoid fitting inaccuracies.
Midpoint calculation is executed correctly; the student shows command of basic geometry. Ensure all values are carried consistently to the final equation.
The perpendicular bisector slope is found correctly; adding the exact fraction before decimal approximation would improve mathematical precision.
The simultaneous solve for the circle center is concise but omits key algebraic steps; include intermediate transformations to maintain clarity.
The radius calculation is thorough; to deepen critical reflection, discuss how rounding to three significant figures may affect the arcâs fit.
Domain restriction via $$y\le -0.506x-2.285$$ is well applied; verify that the inequality sign is consistent when graphing to avoid misrepresenting the arc.
Table 1âs screenshot effectively consolidates arc equations and sample points; the image is slightly blurredâexport at higher resolution for clarity.
The descriptive graphs on this page aid understanding, but they lack captions explaining key featuresâadd concise labels to each subplot.
Multiple graphical representations are used effectively; consider referencing each representation explicitly in the narrative to guide the reader.
Explanation for choosing x=3.5 as a midpoint is pragmatic, but evaluate how this approximation affects the circle fit quantitatively (e.g., measure residual).
The rationale for using polynomial regression is well connected to local extrema; explicitly state the number of maxima/minima to justify degree choices.
The discussion on polynomial degree assumptions is insightful; including a brief discussion of overfitting risks when increasing degree would deepen critical reflection.
The GaussâJordan procedure is explained clearly, but direct quoting from PennState could be paraphrased to demonstrate deeper understanding.
The criteria for reduced row echelon form are stated well; adding a small example of a non-compliant matrix would illustrate common pitfalls.
Fig. 3 effectively shows the matrix after initial row operations; consider shading pivot columns to direct the readerâs attention.
The advanced row operations in Fig. 4 are well documented; verify each numeric result against manual computation to guard against transcription errors.
The partial matrix in Fig. 5 still has nonzero off-pivot entries; a fully reduced echelon form requires clearing these for consistent logical structure.
Comparing manual and Desmos results is excellent practice; include specific RÂ² or residual statistics to quantitatively validate curve fits.
The final compilation of regression curves illustrates the investigationâs scope; add brief captions for each to clarify which function corresponds to each segment.
